gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/tph1/enab.snippet: tph-1 resolved via Alliance gene search

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/tph1/mole.snippet: Existing GO annotation: tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity, IDA

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/tph1/part.snippet: Golr annotation doc: serotonin biosynthetic process, ISS evidence

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/tph1/occu.justification: Figure 1 panel E places tph-1 inside the labeled NEURONS box; CL:0000540 (neuron) is the canonical cell-type term.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/mod1/mole.snippet: Ranganathan, Cannon & Horvitz 2000 — mod-1 encodes a serotonin-gated chloride channel.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/mod1/occu.justification: Figure shows mod-1 in the NEURONS box alongside tph-1.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/nhr76/mol.snippet: Existing GO annotation: nuclear receptor transcription factor activity

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/nhr76/par.snippet: Existing GO annotation: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/nhr76/occ.justification: Figure shows nhr-76 inside the labeled INTESTINE compartment; CL:0000584 (enterocyte) is the canonical intestinal cell-type term.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/atgl1/mol.snippet: Human PNPLA2 (the ATGL ortholog) carries an experimental annotation to triacylglycerol lipase activity; transferred by orthology.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/atgl1/par.snippet: Reactome 'Triglyceride catabolism' pathway includes PNPLA2 (ATGL ortholog) performing this step.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/atgl1/occ.justification: Figure shows atgl-1 inside the INTESTINE compartment downstream of nhr-76.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/tph1/caus.snippet: tph-1 supplies the serotonin signal that mod-1 responds to (Ranganathan et al. 2000).

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/mod1/caus.justification: Figure 1 panel E draws an 'endocrine signal' arrow from the NEURONS compartment (containing mod-1) to the INTESTINE compartment (containing nhr-76); the directionality is shown but no direct molecular link is annotated in GO yet.

gomodel:demo-celegans-fat-loss/nhr76/cau.snippet: Confirmed during curator review: nhr-76 (TF) indirectly positively regulates atgl-1 in C. elegans via transcriptional control — consistent with the figure and published expression data. Indirect because transcription/translation sit between the TF activity and the target's lipase activity.
